role: proof

---

**Theorem E.** Let $\{f_{ij}\}$ be a countable dense set in the metric space of integrable functions measurable with respect to the invariant $\sigma$-ring $\mathbf{T}$ generated by the class of all Baire sets. For each $i,j$, let $C_{ij}$ be a Baire set on which $f_{ij}$ is essentially concentrated. Let $\mathbf{T}$ be the invariant $\sigma$-ring generated by the countable class $\{C_{ij}\}$.

Then it follows from Theorem D that $Y$ is a compact invariant subgroup of $X$ and it follows from 61.A that $Y$ is a Baire set.

Since each $f_{ij}$ is measurable $(\mathbf{T})$, Theorem C implies that $f_{ij}(y^{-1}x) = f_{ij}(x)$ for every $y$ in $Y$ and every $x$ in $X$. It follows that $\chi_{C_i}(y^{-1}x) = \chi_{C_i}(x)$, i.e. that $yC_i = C_i$, for every $y$ in $Y$ and every $i = 1, 2, \dots$. Since, for each $y$ in $Y$, the class of all those sets $F$ for which $yF = F$ is a $\sigma$-ring, it follows that $yE = E$ for every $y$ in $Y$ and every Baire set $E$. Hence $E = YE = \bigcup_{x \in E} Yx$; that is, $E$ is a union of cosets of the invariant subgroup $Y$. $\blacksquare$
